safeguardingLog.narrative: Beatrice disclosed on 30 March 2026 that they had been smacked at home over the weekend. Immediate advice was sought from children's social care.
Staff noticed Imogen arriving hungry and without a packed lunch on 6 April 2026. A referral to early help was made to address financial hardship.
Jude was seen on 2 April 2026 with a sharp object in the playground. Increased monitoring and pastoral support were put in place.
Staff noticed Maya arriving hungry and without a packed lunch on 28 February 2026. A referral to early help was made to address financial hardship.
Oscar mentioned on 3 March 2026 carrying an object 'for protection', later found to be a tool. Increased monitoring and pastoral support were put in place.
Persistent lateness for Mia was raised on 1 April 2026, with arrivals after registration on most mornings this half-term. An attendance support plan was agreed with the family.
Ella raised on 11 March 2026 that unwanted comments of a sexual nature have continued between lessons. A harmful-behaviour plan and pastoral support were put in place.
Repeated late collections of Toby by an adult appearing intoxicated were noted around 12 January 2026. Multi-agency support is being coordinated for the family.

Class teacher noted that Jack's appearance had deteriorated over the half-term, with matted hair and body odour, on 4 January 2026. Pastoral staff are monitoring and supporting the pupil's daily hygiene needs.
An incident of inappropriate contact involving Max was logged on 2 April 2026. The DSL is overseeing follow-up and monitoring.
Staff report that Maya's outbursts have grown more frequent and intense over the past fortnight, logged on 4 January 2026. A reflection conversation was held and the behaviour support plan reviewed.
A lunchtime supervisor witnessed an inappropriate physical incident involving George on 15 February 2026. The pupils were separated and supervision increased.
A series of disruptive incidents involving Daisy was recorded across the week of 24 April 2026. Pastoral staff are providing daily check-ins.
Phoebe alluded on 25 December 2025 to adults at home being 'unwell' in ways linked to substance misuse. The pupil was offered additional pastoral support.
Parents reported on 21 March 2026 that Finley has been deliberately excluded from games by a group of pupils. A restorative meeting was arranged and pastoral support offered to the victim.
Persistent body odour and an unwashed uniform were observed for Thomas across the week of 6 February 2026. A sensitive conversation was held with parents and discreet washing facilities offered.
Evie's sibling shared on 11 April 2026 that the family is struggling financially. The family was signposted to a local food bank and free school meals.
Ivy mentioned on 26 March 2026 carrying an object 'for protection', later found to be a tool. The DSL recorded the incident and considered a referral.
Charlie has been going missing from home for short periods, raised on 12 April 2026. A multi-agency meeting was convened to assess exploitation risk.
Grace has been visibly grieving following a bereavement reported on 27 February 2026. Pastoral check-ins were arranged for the coming weeks.
Concerns were raised on 11 December 2025 about Imogen being contacted by an unknown adult through an online game. The pupil received targeted online-safety education.
Hannah disclosed to the ICT teacher on 7 February 2026 that an online contact had requested photographs. The incident was logged and monitoring put in place.

Florence arrived on 9 March 2026 with matted, unwashed hair and clothing that had not been changed for several days. A sensitive conversation was held with parents and discreet washing facilities offered.
Maisie's grandparent passed away on 22 April 2026, and staff have noticed periods of low mood since. The class teacher will monitor for distress and offer counsellor access.
A small knife was found in William's bag during a check on 1 March 2026. The item was confiscated and a behavioural plan with home involvement agreed.
On 5 November 2025, Imogen left the classroom without permission and became distressed in the corridor. Pastoral staff are providing daily check-ins.
Unexplained cash and new items were noticed with Theo around 12 April 2026. Information was shared with the police safeguarding team.
Poppy arrived on 24 December 2025 with matted, unwashed hair and clothing that had not been changed for several days. School provided spare uniform and a private space to freshen up.
A small knife was found in Arthur's bag during a check on 1 April 2026. A risk assessment was completed and parents informed.
On 19 January 2026, Noah left the classroom without permission and became distressed in the corridor. Pastoral staff are providing daily check-ins.
Lunchtime staff raised concerns on 6 April 2026 about Freya's body odour and visibly unclean clothes. An early help conversation was started with the family about home circumstances.
Freya disclosed on 25 April 2026 that they have been the target of repeated name-calling by classmates. Parents were informed and an anti-bullying plan put in place.
A small knife was found in Jacob's bag during a check on 26 April 2026. A conversation about safety and consequences was held with the pupil.
Aria has been going missing from home for short periods, raised on 4 February 2026. An immediate referral was made regarding contextual safeguarding risk.
Poppy appeared tired and withdrawn on 29 December 2025, later linked to disturbances at home. A calm, safe space was offered to the pupil during the day.
Edward disclosed on 20 April 2026 that they regularly care for a parent with mobility difficulties before school. Pastoral check-ins and practical support were put in place.
A lunchtime supervisor witnessed an inappropriate physical incident involving Ella on 28 March 2026. Statements were taken and a behavioural plan agreed for those involved.
Concerns about conflict in Theo's household were raised on 20 April 2026. Pastoral support was offered and the situation logged.
Ava has shown a marked withdrawal from peers since half-term, with low mood noted on 2 November 2025. The family was signposted to local mental health support.
Class teacher noted that Theo's appearance had deteriorated over the half-term, with matted hair and body odour, on 4 March 2026. Support was offered to the family alongside continued monitoring.
Jacob disclosed on 27 March 2026 that they had been smacked at home over the weekend. A statutory referral to children's social care was submitted.
Archie was seen on 9 December 2025 with a sharp object in the playground. The DSL recorded the incident and considered a referral.
A home visit on 1 December 2025 confirmed financial pressures affecting Ruby's household. Discreet provision of breakfast and snacks was put in place.
Mother contacted school on 28 December 2025 to disclose her own struggles with substance dependency. Multi-agency support is being coordinated for the family.
Maisie told a trusted adult on 30 January 2026 that other children have been mocking them in class and online. A restorative meeting was arranged and pastoral support offered to the victim.
Oscar was absent without notification for three consecutive days from 6 December 2025, prompting a welfare check. The education welfare officer has been asked to follow up.

Persistent lateness for Isla was raised on 18 January 2026, with arrivals after registration on most mornings this half-term. Targeted pastoral support was put in place to encourage regular attendance.
A peer reported on 7 March 2026 that Max had shared a screenshot of an inappropriate message. An online safety conversation was held and parents informed.
Theo's attendance has fallen to around 76% across the spring term, with several unexplained absences flagged on 24 November 2025. Parents were contacted and transport difficulties discussed.
Felix has missed a number of mornings since 1 December 2025, often linked to difficulties getting to school. Parents were contacted and transport difficulties discussed.
Charlie was seen on 23 April 2026 with a sharp object in the playground. A conversation about safety and consequences was held with the pupil.
Parent revealed on 29 March 2026 that Thomas takes on significant caring responsibilities for a younger sibling. The family was offered early help to reduce the caring burden.
A pattern of Monday and Friday absences for Isla was identified during the week of 11 January 2026. An attendance support plan was agreed with the family.
Mother shared on 10 March 2026 that a parent has a serious illness, which is affecting Lucas. Bereavement support resources were shared with the family.
Erin was found on 13 December 2025 to have accessed age-inappropriate content on a school device. A police safeguarding referral was made as a precaution.
Class teacher observed Mia repeatedly asking peers for spare food at lunch during the week of 1 April 2026. Discreet provision of breakfast and snacks was put in place.
A visible mark was noted on Noah on 9 March 2026 during a routine activity. The injury was recorded with a body map and reported.
A lunchtime supervisor noticed James sitting alone repeatedly during the week of 23 February 2026, later linked to being picked on. The pupil is receiving check-ins while the situation is monitored.
On 13 February 2026, Freya left the classroom without permission and became distressed in the corridor. The SENCo has been consulted about possible underlying needs.
